rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a material internal contradiction: the central claim asserts that semantic constraints (well-typedness, termination) are sufficient to resolve ambiguity, yet the ablation shows removing the type-check filter costs only 1.8 accuracy points while removing the PCFG ranking drops accuracy by ~59 points. A general 'look at' of a technical paragraph naturally encompasses checking whether its reasoning holds together, making logical consistency a central—though not unambiguously load-bearing—responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The ablation numbers reported (−1.8 pts for type-check removal; −59 pts for uniform-distribution substitution) are the primary empirical evidence in the artifact and directly determine whether the stated thesis is supported. Any open-ended review of this passage would be expected to flag whether the conclusion drawn matches what the data actually show, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is a common idiom for prose or copyedit review; writing polish is a plausibly load-bearing interpretation of the delegation that cannot be ruled out by the text alone.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking primarily for feedback on the prose and writing clarity, or on whether the argument and empirical evidence (including the ablation results) actually support the paper's central claims?
